checks.a_analysis: The conversion results are derived directly from the source material. Section headings like 'Project Timeline' are reasonably inferred from document structure or existing list headers. The addition of the word 'deliverables' in the Table 5 caption is a contextual completion of an abruptly ending sentence in the source and falls under acceptable AI-generated captioning/normalization.
The source document contains no phone numbers, emails, or URLs, and the output correctly reflects this absence.
All three pages of the source PDF are represented in the output sections. No entire pages or primary content sections were dropped.
Table 5 in the source document contains one data row and several header/sub-header rows. The JSON output faithfully captures the single data row and correctly structures the complex headers.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Significant technical information is missing from the first two sections. In Section 1 (Page 1), the report's reference date 'April 9, 2024 NCEM Status' and the detailed map legend (defining cell sizes like 3, 5, 10, 20, and 50ft) are missing from the text elements. In Section 2 (Page 2), the entire 'Phase & Quality' legend (defining years and Quality Levels 1-2 for all five phases) is omitted. These omissions constitute over 25% of the unique informational text on these pages.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1: 'April 9, 2024 NCEM Status' and Status legend ('Reprojected from UTM meter to State Plane feet *', 'DEMs in Que', 'DEMs Processing (3, 5, 10, 20, 50ft)', 'DEMs Created (3, 10, & 20ft Tiled)'). Page 2: 'Phase & Quality' legend details ('Phase 1 (2020) - QL 1', 'Phase 2 (2020) - QL 1', 'Phase 3 (2015) - QL 2', 'Phase 4 (2016) - QL 1', 'Phase 5 (2017) - QL 1').
checks.b_evidence_output: (These technical details are entirely absent from the text and paragraphs of sections 1 and 2)

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The agency branding 'NC DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC SAFETY' and 'Emergency Management', which appears prominently at the top of every page in the source, is entirely missing from the output text and metadata. While the main document content is prioritized, the total loss of authoring agency identity is a defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: NC DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC SAFETY Emergency Management

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the entire document and compared the source PDF text with the output JSON. No content duplication was found. The title '2020 United States Geological Survey LiDAR' appears as both the document metadata title and the first section heading, which is expected behavior. Body text, table contents, and list items are unique and appear only once.

The heading hierarchy is logical. The document title is mapped to Section 1's title. Sub-sections like 'NC Hurricane Florence...' on Page 1 are correctly identified as H2. Section titles for Pages 2 and 3 are also treated correctly. There are no skipped levels (e.g., H1 to H3).

Table 5 on page 3 has been faithfully converted. The source table features a merged header 'Delivery dates' spanning two columns. The JSON has correctly flattened this by prepending 'Delivery dates:' to the respective column headers, which preserves the structural context for screen readers. The column count (3) and row count (2 rows of content) match the source.

The reading order in the output JSON matches the logical flow of the source PDF. It proceeds page-by-page, capturing titles, maps, text boxes, and tables in the order they are presented to a human reader.

The bulleted item on Page 3 ('Project Timeline') is correctly identified and structured as an unordered list. The asterisked notes on Page 1 are rendered as paragraphs; given their use of non-standard markers (* and **), this is a reasonable interpretation of their function as annotations rather than a formal list.
There are no hyperlinks present in the source PDF. No links were incorrectly injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The source document is primarily a visual status report, with each page organized around a large map of North Carolina. In the HTML version, the first map (from PDF page 1) is completely missing, and the second map (from PDF page 2) is rendered as a solid green square. This drastically alters the visual organization and flow of the document, as the text is no longer visually associated with the geographic data it describes.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF (pages 1 and 2) uses large maps to provide the primary context for the status and quality level data.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output omits the first map entirely and displays the second map as an uninformative solid green square, leaving the text in a single column without its visual references.

checks.a_analysis: The document contains one table (Table 5). In the HTML output, this table is rendered correctly with clearly defined borders, distinct headers, and readable content. The visual structure matches the intent of the source table.

Several meaningful visual elements are missing or broken in the HTML output. The status map on page 1 and the diagram showing a plane performing LiDAR collection on page 2 are entirely absent. The quality level map on page 2 is present as a container but is rendered as a solid bright green square, losing all its informational content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Map of 'April 9, 2024 NCEM Status' on page 1; map of 'North Carolina LiDAR Quality Level By Phase' and diagram of plane with LiDAR scanner on page 2.
checks.b_evidence_output: The map from page 1 and the plane diagram are missing. The map from page 2 appears as a solid green square.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of body content duplication in the HTML output. Each section of text and the table appears only once in the main flow of the document.
